summary: Task 02 verbose toggle plumbing is implemented faithfully to the tech-spec (Decision 2) and the task file. It correctly mirrors the showExtra write-into-all-views persistence pattern, adds the no-Reset verbose change-detection branch in collectStat() ahead of both Reset paths, and is covered by a test modeled on Test_toggleIdleConns. Build, go vet, and the new Test_toggleVerbose all pass; no view-count or keybinding regressions.

suggestions.suggestion: There are now two consecutive change-detection branches (verbose at ~99-106 and CollectExtra at ~108-116) that each end in `continue` or a Reset. The ordering invariant 'verbose branch must precede both Reset paths' is load-bearing and only documented in code comments. Consider a brief grouping comment above the ShowExtra branch noting the intentional order (refresh -> ShowExtra -> Verbose -> CollectExtra -> unconditional Reset) so a future edit does not accidentally move the verbose early-out below a Reset.
suggestions.benefit: Protects the single highest-risk invariant of the task (no delta-blanking on toggle) against future reordering during later-wave edits to the same hot file.

suggestions.suggestion: The test uses config.views["activity"] as the active view but does not assert that toggling verbose leaves config.view.Name unchanged, nor that a subsequent viewSwitchHandler preserves Verbose (the persistence-across-switch AC). The map-entry assertion (lines 46-48) covers the mechanism, but an explicit 'switch view, flag still set' assertion would lock the persistence AC directly rather than by proxy.
suggestions.benefit: Directly verifies acceptance criterion 'verbose flag persists across screen switches' instead of inferring it from the views-map write, hardening against a future viewSwitchHandler change.
